metadata.title: Dario Amodei Prediction Tracker
metadata.description: A single source of truth tracking Dario Amodei's (Anthropic CEO) public AI predictions, validated against primary sources and current events.

status_legend.Pending: Target date in the future; not yet verifiable.
status_legend.Achieved: Prediction verifiably came true on or before target date.
status_legend.Partial: Partially realized; some but not all conditions met.
status_legend.Walked Back: Predictor publicly retracted, softened, or revised the claim.

predictions.prediction: "Powerful AI" (a 'country of geniuses in a datacenter') could arrive as early as 2026 in the optimistic scenario.
predictions.quote: I think it could come as early as 2026... a country of geniuses in a datacenter.
predictions.notes: Defines 'powerful AI' as smarter than a Nobel laureate across most fields, with the agency, tools, and speed of millions of copies. Seed entry.

predictions.prediction: Powerful AI systems will emerge in late 2026 or early 2027, with intellectual capabilities matching or exceeding Nobel Prize winners across most disciplines.
predictions.quote: we expect powerful AI systems will emerge in late 2026 or early 2027... matching or exceeding that of Nobel Prize winners across most disciplines.

source.title: Anthropic Recommendations to OSTP for the U.S. AI Action Plan

predictions.notes: Formal written policy submission; the most concrete dated AGI-timeline commitment on record.

predictions.prediction: A 'country of geniuses in a datacenter' (~50 million AI agents, each smarter than a Nobel laureate, running at 10-100x human speed) could materialize by 2027.
predictions.quote: a country of geniuses in a datacenter
predictions.notes: Companion essay to Machines of Loving Grace, published Jan 2026; reaffirms the ~2027 powerful-AI window while emphasizing five risk categories.

predictions.prediction: AI will be writing 90% of all code within 3-6 months, and essentially all code within 12 months.
predictions.quote: I think we'll be there in three to six months, where AI is writing 90 percent of the code. And then, in 12 months, we may be in a world where AI is writing essentially all of the code.

source.title: Council on Foreign Relations event (reported)
source.publisher: Council on Foreign Relations / Yahoo Finance

predictions.notes: The 3-6 month window (Sep 2025) passed without the 90%-of-all-code threshold being met industry-wide. Amodei later softened to 'on many teams.' Independent analyses (Redwood Research) found the claim unverified even within Anthropic.

predictions.prediction: AI could compress 50-100 years of biological progress into 5-10 years, potentially doubling the human lifespan.
predictions.quote: I think AI could help us compress the next 50 to 100 years of biological progress into 5 to 10 years.
predictions.notes: The 'compressed 21st century' thesis. Reasoning: 20th-century life expectancy roughly doubled (~40 to ~75); a compressed 21st could double it again toward ~150. Long-horizon; remains Pending.

predictions.prediction: AI could eliminate up to 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs within five years and push unemployment to 10-20%.
predictions.quote: AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white-collar jobs and spike unemployment to 10-20% in the next one to five years.

source.title: Axios interview: 'Behind the Curtain: A white-collar bloodbath'

predictions.notes: By May 2026, Amodei publicly tempered this forecast (Fortune), suggesting automation may expand work rather than purely destroy it. Yale Budget Lab found no meaningful unemployment shift for AI-exposed workers through early 2026, though entry-level tech hiring did fall sharply.
